steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Extract the radius from the diameter
steps.content:
The diameter is 8 inches. Since radius is half the diameter:
$$r = \frac{8}{2} = 4 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Calculate the volume using the cylinder formula
steps.content:
Use the cylinder volume formula with $r = 4$ and $h = 15$:
$$V = \pi(4)^2(15) = \pi(16)(15) = 240\pi \text{ cubic inches}$$
That confirms C is correct.
